briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for May 24, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Domestic and International Climate Emergency and Health Threats
sections.content: This summer, 'climate adaptation' is urgent domestically [1], and an 'unprecedented heatwave' approaching 30 degrees Celsius is expected to hit the Korean Peninsula starting in May [2]. Internationally, severe conditions have also been observed, with some parts of the UK reaching heatwave thresholds and recording the hottest May in 80 years, with London hitting 32.3°C [24]. These climate changes are increasing the risks of cancer, heat stress, and infectious diseases [21], and a climate change risk report has revealed significant public health policy gaps [17]. Meanwhile, there was also a statement from an energy minister claiming that global warming 'is not even close to being among the world's top 5 or 10 problems' [23].

sections.title: ⚠️ New Environmental Issues and Changes in Climate Scenarios
sections.content: Recently, rocket launches have surged due to increased demand for constellation satellites for internet activation, such as SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket launching with 29 Starlink satellites, resulting in 329 launches last year alone. This is emerging as a new concern with adverse effects on the climate [11]. In positive news, the IPCC (Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change) has announced the abandonment of the worst-case climate change scenarios [14], signifying the end of extreme global warming scenarios [15]. Furthermore, a study published in 'Science' in 2019 claimed that natural forest restoration is the 'best climate change solution,' raising expectations for nature-based solutions [25].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Domestic Climate Adaptation and Carbon Neutrality Practices
sections.content: Domestically, efforts to respond to climate crisis disasters and achieve carbon neutrality are actively continuing. Seongbuk-gu is fully operating its climate crisis disaster response strengthening project [3], and Seongdong-gu is progressing well, having received an 'Excellent' rating in the 2025 implementation review for its carbon neutrality and climate crisis adaptation measures [4, 9]. Jeongeup City is transforming its roads, industries, and overall administration to transition into a carbon-neutral city [5], and the Jeju Environmental Education Center has been selected as the next operating institution for the 'Carbon Neutrality Practice Education Platform,' strengthening its educational foundation [6]. Concurrently, the Geumho-dong housing complex plans a major transformation into a carbon-neutral national strategic research complex, and corporate carbon neutrality practices are also continuing, with Hansung Motors planting 600 Korean fir trees at Hoengseong Forest Retreat [7, 8].

sections.title: 🌍 Various Discussions and Reflections on Climate Change Response
sections.content: In the field of climate change, various discussions and reflections are underway. Rafe Pomerance, known as the 'Paul Revere' of climate change, has passed away at the age of 79, prompting a reflection on his contributions [13]. Meanwhile, questions are being raised about who will succeed in the 'frontline' experiments of renewable energy, indicating concerns about the direction of future energy transition [10]. Scientists are also continuing to explore innovative solutions, such as proposing the construction of a massive dam in the Bering Strait to slow down the effects of climate change [18].

briefing.closing: This concludes today's climate briefing for May 24, 2026. We look forward to seeing you at the next briefing.

articles.original_title: 누가 지구 머리 위에 X 쐈어? 기후에 악영향 끼치는 ‘위성 발사’
articles.description: On the 1st (local time), a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 29 Starlink satellites ascended towards Earth's orbit from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida, USA. (UPI Yonhap News) The surging demand for constellation satellites to enhance internet connectivity has led to a sharp increase in rocket launches, with 329 engine operations last year alone. The carbon particulate matter produced has a global warming potential 540 times higher than that on the ground, increasing stratospheric temperatures and blocking sunlight.

summary.phenomenon: On the 1st (local time), a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 29 Starlink satellites ascended towards Earth's orbit from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida, USA. The increasing demand for constellation satellites to enhance internet connectivity has led to a surge in rocket launches, with 329 launches recorded last year alone.
summary.cause: Carbon particulate matter generated during rocket engine operation has a global warming potential 540 times higher than that on the ground.
summary.outlook: This carbon particulate matter could increase stratospheric temperatures and block sunlight.

articles.original_description: 지난 1일(현지시간) 미국 플로리다주 케이프커내버럴 우주군 기지에서 스페이스X의 팰컨9 로켓이 스타링크용 위성 29기를 싣고 지구 궤도를 향해 상승하고 있다. UPI연합뉴스인터넷 활성화용 군집 위성 수요에 ‘로켓 발사’ 급증…지난해만 329회 엔진 작동하며 나오는 탄소 찌꺼기 지상보다 온난화 유발 능력 ‘540배’ 성층권 온도 높이고 태양광은 차단# 가까운···
